meta.method: end-to-end read of data/razorpay_tos.md; no regex

clauses.title: Chargeback definition
clauses.verbatim_text: “Chargeback” shall mean the reversal (such reversal being requested by a Facility Provider pursuant to a request from the Facility Provider's customer) of the debit of the Transaction Amount that was charged by You, where the reversal is approved by the Facility Provider following examination of the Transaction related documents and information furnished by You, consequently resulting in Razorpay PA being charged the Transaction Amount and charges, penalties or fines associated with processing the Chargeback.

clauses.title: Escrow Account definition
clauses.verbatim_text: “Escrow Account” is an account held by Razorpay PA with an Escrow Bank for the purpose of receiving the Transaction Amount and effecting settlements to You including the inward collection account and the outward collection account.

clauses.title: Facility Providers definition
clauses.verbatim_text: “Facility Providers” shall means banks, financial institutions, NPCI, technology service providers, or other third parties facilitating the provisions of Services or any part thereof, including but not limited to (a) acquiring banks, (b) banks issuing credit cards, debit cards, prepaid instruments and accounts, and (c) Card Networks.

clauses.title: KYC Guidelines definition
clauses.verbatim_text: “KYC Guidelines” means KYC norms as set out in the Master Direction - Know Your Customer, 2016 notified by Reserve Bank of India through circular no. Master Direction DBR.AML.BC.No.81/14.01.001/2015-16, to the extent applicable to the Services and as may be determined by Razorpay PA or the Facility Providers including any amendments, clarifications, FAQs, etc. which may be issued from time to time.. KYC means know-your-customer.

clauses.title: Permissible Deductions definition
clauses.verbatim_text: “Permissible Deductions” means (a) fees charged by Razorpay PA; (b) Chargeback Amount including fines and penalties; and (c) any other sum due and payable by You to Razorpay PA.

clauses.title: Refund definition
clauses.verbatim_text: “Refund” means processing of Your request to Razorpay PA, for returning the Transaction Amount (or part thereof) to the Payment Instrument which was used for effecting the payment of the Transaction Amount.

clauses.title: Ownership of Services / Platform / marks
clauses.verbatim_text: 1.1 We (and our successors and licensors, as applicable) remain the sole owner of all rights, title and interest in the Services, including the Platform, and all underlying intellectual property rights (whether registered or not). Razorpay PA grants You a personal, revocable, non-exclusive, non-transferable, limited right to access the Platform and make use of the Platform and the Services in accordance with the Terms. You shall not remove, obscure, or alter any proprietary rights notices (including trademark and copyright notices), which may be affixed to or contained within the Services. We reserve all rights not granted under the Terms. We (and our successors and licensors, as applicable) retain our rights in and to trademarks, trade names, service marks, logos, domain names, and other distinctive brand features (“marks”) owned or used by us in the course of our business. You do not have the right to use any of our marks without explicit consent from us. You shall not download, copy, create a derivative work, modify, reverse engineer, reverse assemble, transmit or otherwise attempt to discover any source code, sell, assign, sub-license, grant a security interest in or otherwise transfer any right in the Services or marks. You further acknowledge and agree that the Services may contain information that is designated confidential by us and You shall not disclose such information without our prior written consent.

clauses.title: License grant from Merchant for use of materials
clauses.verbatim_text: 1.2 You grant a royalty-free, non-exclusive, irrevocable, transferable and sub-licensable license to Razorpay PA, the Group Entities and third-party service providers, to use Your data, Your customer’s data, information, content, trademarks, logos and any other materials/information You upload or make available to us or on the Platform (“**Your materials**”). You agree that Razorpay PA may use Your materials to operate and improve the Platform and/or the Services and fulfil Razorpay PA’s rights and discharge its obligations under the Terms, including providing the Services. You agree that Razorpay PA may use Your materials in its marketing and promotional materials without requiring any incremental consent from You. You further agree that Razorpay PA may conduct analytics on Your materials and that Razorpay PA shall retain ownership of the results or reports derived from such data (which shall be in aggregated and anonymised form) for its business purposes in accordance with Applicable Laws. You shall indemnify and hold harmless Razorpay PA, the Group Entities and its service providers on demand against all claims and losses arising out of or in connection with our use of Your Materials in accordance with this clause.

clauses.title: Registration eligibility and Secure Credentials responsibility
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.1 You shall register to become a user of the Services and/or the Platform only if You are of the age of 18 or above and possess the legal capacity and full authority to enter into and perform binding contracts as per Applicable Laws. You are responsible for maintaining the secrecy of all Your passwords, keys, security codes, login and account information (“**Secure Credentials**”) associated with Your account with Razorpay PA and API access. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of the Secure Credentials. You will be responsible for the use of the Platform and/ or Services by You or anyone using Your Secure Credentials including but not limited to any unauthorized access, fraudulent transactions, or loss of data resulting from Your failure to maintain the confidentiality of Your Secure Credentials. You are solely responsible for all activities that occur under Your account/in using Your Secure Credentials and Razorpay PA shall not be liable for any such change or action performed by using Your Secure Credentials on the Website.

clauses.title: Accuracy of KYC / business info; suspension on false info
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.2 You shall ensure that all information provided to Razorpay PA (as and when prompted by the Platform) including, but not limited to KYC Documents, financial statements, business details, and contact information is true, accurate, current and complete. If You provide any information that is untrue, inaccurate, not updated or incomplete (or becomes untrue, inaccurate or incomplete), or if Razorpay PA has reasonable grounds to suspect that such information is untrue, inaccurate, not updated or incomplete, Razorpay PA shall have the right to immediately suspend Services and/or terminate Your account and/or refuse any and all current or future use of the Platform or Services, or any portion thereof, in connection thereto.

clauses.title: Customer due diligence and CKYCR retrieval consent
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.7 You agree and covenant that before the commencement of any Service(s) and on an ongoing basis thereafter, as part of Razorpay PA’s mandatory customer due-diligence (CDD) process, Razorpay PA shall have the absolute right to retrieve Your records and documents (as determined in Razorpay PA's sole discretion or when required by Facility Providers or governmental authorities or law enforcement agencies) (collectively “KYC Documents”) from the Central KYC Records (“CKYCR”) without any intimation or pre-approval from You in accordance with Applicable Laws. You expressly consent to this retrieval in accordance with Applicable Laws. In the event Your KYC Documents are not available in CKYCR, or if the retrieved record is not updated or is incomplete, You shall provide the necessary documents to enable Razorpay PA to conduct/complete the due diligence in respect of You and Your business / activities. Razorpay PA shall have the right to share the KYC Documents (or the information therein) and other related documents with the Facility Providers or governmental authorities or law enforcement agencies or the Financial Intelligence Unit-India (FIU-IND) , as required under the Applicable Laws. You expressly consent to Razorpay PA relying on the KYC Documents provided by You for providing Services. You further acknowledge and agree that Razorpay PA reserves the right at all times to monitor, review, retain and/or disclose any information in relation to the Service(s) as necessary to satisfy any Applicable Laws, legal process or governmental request. You agree to promptly update Razorpay PA of any change in Your Information, promptly upon such change taking effect.

clauses.title: Additional KYC documents; suspension for non-submission
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.8 Razorpay PA shall have the right to demand from You, any (i) additional KYC Documents and /or (ii) any KYC related or other documents of Your customers or invoices, in its sole discretion and / or as per the Applicable Laws or pursuant to requests from governmental authorities, law enforcement agencies or Facility Providers. Your failure to submit the KYC Documents when requisitioned shall entitle Razorpay PA to suspend the Services and/or stop settlement of monies (as applicable) until You submit such KYC Documents to the sole satisfaction of Razorpay PA.

clauses.title: Razorpay not responsible for delivery / after-sales / refund handling
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.9 You agree that Razorpay PA shall neither be responsible for any delivery and after-sales service requests, pertaining to the goods and services offered by You, nor for any refund requests , invoicing or collection, customer enquiries (not limited to sales enquiries), technical support maintenance services and/or any other obligations or services relating to or in respect of Your products or services. Such obligations shall be Your sole responsibility. You shall indemnify Razorpay PA against any claim arising from such services or obligations and shall bear any and all expenses and/or costs relating thereto.

clauses.title: Representations; crypto / NFT prohibition and indemnity
clauses.verbatim_text:
2.13 You represent, undertake and warrant that

You are duly incorporated or established under the laws of Your jurisdiction and have all requisite power and authority to own and operate Your business.

You have the full legal capacity and power to enter into, exercise Your rights under, and perform Your obligations under these Terms.

The execution, delivery and performance of these Terms has been authorized by all necessary corporate and organizational actions including but not limited to board resolution and/or power of attorney and/or letter of authority to bind Your business and Your company/firm/organization.

You have duly accepted these Terms, which form a legal, valid and binding obligation, enforceable in accordance with its clauses.

You do not, and shall not, engage in any activity related to virtual currency, cryptocurrency and other crypto products (like non-fungible tokens or NFTs), prohibited investments for commercial gain or credits that can be monetized, re-sold or converted to physical or digital goods or services or otherwise exit the virtual world. Any breach of this provision shall be subject to immediate suspension or termination of Your access to or use of the Platform or any or all Services, at Razorpay PA's sole discretion. You shall indemnify Razorpay PA from any losses arising from Your breach of this provision.

clauses.title: Record retention of 10 years and audit cooperation
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.18 You shall assist Razorpay PA in furnishing to its auditors, the Facility Providers, governmental authorities, or law enforcement agencies, forthwith upon request from time to time, KYC Documents, relevant books, the original copy / copies of proof of transactions, invoices or other records, including pertaining to any order placed by Your customers. You shall retain records relating to transactions for a period of 10 (ten) years from the relevant date of the order placed on Your site. Razorpay PA and the Facility Providers, and/or governmental agencies and/or law enforcement agencies shall be entitled to audit and inspect the records and other data relating to the customer's orders at any time whatsoever and without any prior notice. You shall ensure cooperation with Razorpay PA, its auditors, Facility Providers, governmental authorities, or law enforcement agencies for any audit, inspection or pursuant to any other request.

clauses.title: Convenience fee on customers; Merchant bears losses
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.20 When a customer purchases goods or services from You, You may, in Your sole discretion, impose any convenience fee on the customer. Any responsibility or losses incurred by Razorpay PA as a result of the charge of any such convenience fee by You to Your customers shall be borne by You. You shall also indemnify Razorpay PA for such liability or losses.

clauses.title: Third-party orchestrators require prior written consent
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.21 You shall not engage, appoint, or otherwise utilize any third-party service provider in connection with the Services under this Agreement without the prior written consent of Razorpay PA. You further agree that You shall utilize the Services or Platform (a) directly from Razorpay PA; or (b) through a payment orchestration solution provided or expressly approved by Razorpay PA; or (c) via a proprietary in-house orchestration solution developed by the Merchant, subject to Razorpay PA’s prior written approval. You shall not engage, integrate, or route any payments through a third-party payment orchestrator not explicitly approved by Razorpay PA. Any breach of this clause, including use of an unapproved third-party orchestrator, shall entitle Razorpay PA to immediately suspend and/or terminate the Services without any liability to Razorpay PA.

clauses.title: Recovery of penalties from settlement amounts
clauses.verbatim_text: 2.25 You agree and understand that in case any penalty/other liability is imposed/passed on to Razorpay PA by a law enforcement agency or a regulatory body or Card Association or an institution including a Facility Provider or under Applicable Laws, due to any act or failure to act attributable to You, then Razorpay PA shall be entitled to recover such amounts, including by deducting the amount from Your settlement amount. Without prejudice to any other remedies available to Razorpay PA herein, Razorpay PA may also initiate the requisite proceedings against You to recover such amounts.

clauses.title: Applicable fees; zero MDR for Rupay Debit and UPI
clauses.verbatim_text: 3.1 Applicable fees for the provision of Services/Devices shall be levied by Razorpay PA from time to time. You agree that the fees shall be charged according to the manner, rates and frequency determined by Razorpay PA. Razorpay PA reserves the right to update the amount of the fees charged at its sole discretion. Razorpay PA fees allow access to the entire suite of payments products, dashboard and custom reports, and includes MDR charges, if any, for payment instruments as prescribed under Applicable Law. For clarity, Razorpay PA fees include zero MDR for Rupay Debit Cards and UPI transactions.

clauses.title: Fees exclusive of taxes; statutory variations borne by Merchant
clauses.verbatim_text: 3.2 Fees are exclusive of applicable taxes and Razorpay PA will charge such applicable taxes on the fees from time to time. You agree that any statutory variations in applicable taxes during the subsistence of these Terms shall be borne by You.

clauses.title: Monthly invoices; 10-day dispute window
clauses.verbatim_text: 3.3 We will raise monthly invoices in respect of fees charged for Services provided during such months. Invoices are available on the dashboard on a monthly basis. Any dispute in respect of an invoice must be communicated by You to us via a notice no later than ten (10) days from the date of the invoice, failing which such invoices would be deemed to be accepted by You. For disputed invoices, Razorpay PA shall use good faith efforts to reconcile any reasonably disputed amounts.

clauses.title: Daily reconciliation; 3-day discrepancy reporting
clauses.verbatim_text: 3.4 You shall be responsible for daily reconciliation of all the transactions processed. In case of discrepancies, You shall report to Razorpay PA regarding such discrepancy within three (3) days upon the receipt of the fund settlements. Razorpay PA shall not be liable for any reconciliation issue if the same is highlighted by You to Razorpay PA after such time.

clauses.title: Section 194H TDS / Form 16-A reimbursement
clauses.verbatim_text: 3.5 For fees deducted upfront before provision of the specific Service, it is agreed that if You deposit applicable taxes under Section 194H of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (in respect of invoices received by You) and furnish to Razorpay PA Form 16-A in respect of such taxes paid, then Razorpay PA shall reimburse to You, on a quarterly basis, the amount in respect of such taxes paid. In all other cases, with respect to invoices received by You, at the time of payment of the Fees, You will withhold applicable taxes under Section 194H of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (in case LTDC is provided as per the LTDC issued). You shall deposit the withheld taxes with the government treasury, file the statutorily mandated returns and furnish the requisite tax deduction certificate (Form 16-A) to Razorpay PA within one hundred and eighty (180) days so as to enable Razorpay PA to obtain full credit for the taxes deducted at source.

clauses.title: GST number update responsibility
clauses.verbatim_text: 3.7 You shall be solely responsible for updating Your GST registration number on the Razorpay PA dashboard before Razorpay PA generates the invoice and shall also submit the GST certificate as part of KYC. Razorpay PA will raise a GST tax invoice and report the transactions in the GST returns based on the information provided by You. The GST returns will be filed as per the statutory timelines, to enable You to avail appropriate input tax credit. Razorpay PA shall not be responsible for any mistake and or misrepresentation by You in updating the GST number and other particulars as per the GST certificate. Further, any liability raised on Razorpay PA by the GST authorities due to incorrect information provided by You or deliberate withholding of any statutory information by You shall be recovered by Razorpay PA from You.

clauses.title: Exclusions of damages
clauses.verbatim_text: 7.1 Razorpay PA (including its officers, directors, employees, representatives, Group Entities, and providers) will not be responsible or liable for (a) any injury, death, loss, claim, act of God, accident, delay, or any direct, special, exemplary, punitive, indirect, incidental or consequential damages of any kind (including without limitation lost profits or lost savings), whether based in contract, tort, strict liability or otherwise, that arise out of or is in any way connected with (i) any failure or delay (including without limitation the use of or inability to use any component of the Services and/or Platform), or (ii) any use of the Platform or Services or content therein, or (iii) the performance or non-performance by us or any Facility Provider, even if we have been advised of the possibility of damages to such parties or any other party, or (iv) any damages to or viruses that may infect Your computer equipment or other property as the result of Your access to the Platform or Services or Your use of any content therein.

clauses.title: Liability cap: one month fees
clauses.verbatim_text: 7.2 Notwithstanding anything under these Terms, Razorpay PA's aggregate liability and that of the Group Entities, officers, employees, Facility Providers and agents relating to the Service(s), will not exceed an amount equal to one (1) month fees paid by You for the specific Service(s) giving rise to the liability. Razorpay PA's liability under or in connection with Terms will be proportionately reduced to the extent any loss or damage is contributed to by You or Your third-party providers.

clauses.title: Merchant indemnification of Razorpay and survival
clauses.verbatim_text: 8.1 You agree to indemnify and hold Razorpay PA (and its Facility Providers, officers, the Group Entities, directors, agents and employees) harmless from any and against all claims, whether or not brought by third parties, causes of action, demands, recoveries, losses, damages, fines, penalties or other costs or expenses of any kind or nature, including reasonable attorneys' fees, or arising out of or related to Your breach of these Terms, Your violation of any Applicable Laws or the rights of a third party, or Your use of the Platform, any claim brought by a third party in relation to the goods and/or services sold by You, breach of Your confidentiality obligations, Your integration with Razorpay PA leading to mismatches, frauds, hacking, data leakage or any other harm that Razorpay PA suffers or any disputes between You and any third party. The covenants of indemnity set forth herein shall survive and continue even after the termination of Your use of the Services.

clauses.title: Reimbursement of card network fines; suspension rights
clauses.verbatim_text: 9.3 In the event that Your non-compliance of Card Payment Network Rules, results in any fines, penalties or other amounts being levied on or demanded of us by a card payment network, then without prejudice to our other rights hereunder, You shall forthwith reimburse us in an amount equal to the fines, penalties or other amount so levied or demanded or spent by us in any manner in relation to such fines, penalties and levies. If You fail to comply with Your obligations towards the Card Networks, Razorpay PA may suspend settlement or suspend/terminate the Services forthwith.

clauses.title: Force majeure event definition and excuse from performance
clauses.verbatim_text: 11.1 If performance of Services/Platform by Razorpay PA is prevented, restricted, delayed or interfered with, by reason of labour disputes, strikes, acts of God, epidemic, pandemic, floods, lightning, severe weather, shortages of materials, rationing, inducement of any virus, malware, trojan or other disruptive mechanisms, any event of hacking or illegal usage of the Services or Platform, utility or communication failures, earthquakes, war, revolution, acts of terrorism, civil commotion, acts of public enemies, blockade, embargo or any law, order, proclamation, regulation, ordinance, demand or requirement having legal effect of any government, regulatory or any judicial authority or representative of any such government, or any other act whatsoever, whether similar or dissimilar to those referred to in this clause, which are beyond the reasonable control of Razorpay PA, then Razorpay PA shall be excused and discharged from such performance to the extent of and during the period of such force majeure event, and such non-performance shall, in no manner whosoever, amount to a breach by Razorpay PA of its obligations herein or incur any legal liability on Razorpay PA.

clauses.title: 24-hour breach notification obligation
clauses.verbatim_text: 12.3 You shall immediately notify Razorpay PA, and in no event later than twenty-four (24) hours, upon becoming aware of any actual or suspected data security breach, unauthorized access, loss, misuse, or compromise of Customer Data in Your possession or control, or within Your systems. You shall cooperate fully with Razorpay PA and any regulatory authority or Card Network in the investigation and remediation of any such incident.

clauses.title: Prohibition on storing full card credentials
clauses.verbatim_text: 12.4 You shall not, under any circumstance, store or any sensitive Customer Data, including full card credentials (full card number, CVV, expiry date, or PIN), within Your systems or infrastructure or access any server or database containing or any sensitive Customer Data, including full card credentials (full card number, CVV, expiry date, or PIN).

clauses.title: Governing law (India); courts in Bangalore; set-off; third-party settlement
clauses.verbatim_text:
14.2 Additional terms applicable to the Services are as under:

The laws of India, without regard to its conflict of laws, rules, will govern these Terms, as well as Your and our observance of the same. If You take any legal action relating to Your use of the Services/ Platform or these Terms, You agree to file such action only in the courts located in Bangalore, India. You acknowledge that You have read and have understood these Terms, and that these Terms have the same force and effect as a signed agreement. This clause shall survive termination of the Terms

Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies Razorpay PA may have, You hereby agree and confirm that Razorpay PA shall have the right to set-off by whatever means the whole or any part of Your liability to Razorpay PA under these Terms (or any other agreement between You and Razorpay PA or its Group Entities) against any funds, sums or other amounts credited to, or owing to, You under these Terms (or any other agreement between You and Razorpay PA or its affiliates). You agree that Razorpay PA may exercise the right of set-off at any time, without any prior notice to You. In the event such set off does not fully reimburse Razorpay PA for the liability owed, You shall pay Razorpay PA a sum equal to any shortfall thereof.

You shall not (whether on-line or otherwise): (i) describe Yourself as an agent or representative of Razorpay PA or any Facility Provider; (ii) represent that You have any rights to offer any products or services offered by Razorpay PA or the Facility Provider; and (iii) make any representations to Your customer or any third party or give any warranties which may require Razorpay PA or Facility Provider to undertake to or be liable for, whether directly or indirectly, any obligation and/or responsibility to customer or any third party. (d) Razorpay PA reserves the right to make changes to the website, related policies and agreements, these Terms, Website Terms of Use, and the Privacy Policy at any time as it deems fit and proper, including but not limited to comply with changes in law or regulation, correct inaccuracies, omissions, errors or ambiguities, reflect changes in the process flow, scope and nature of the Services and ancillary services, company reorganization, market practice or customer requirements.

If You require Razorpay to settle the Transaction Amount to a third party, then on Your instructions, Razorpay may make settlement of the Transaction Amount to the respective third party, either fully or in part, and You represent that such third party to whom Razorpay is instructed to make settlements, directly interfaces (as a Payee) with the Customers (as a Payer), in respect of such Transaction. In this regard, You further represent and confirm that You have an annual turnover exceeding ₹40 lakh (or annual export turnover exceeding ₹5 lakh, as applicable) and shall at all times maintain such annual turnover and inform Razorpay immediately, if its annual turnover falls below the said threshold

clauses.title: Additional fees for value-added and new services
clauses.verbatim_text:
14.3 You agree that the fees for any Services under these Terms shall be charged according to the manner, rates and frequency determined by Razorpay PA. Razorpay reserves the right to update the amount of the fees including for Services for which no charge has been levied previously in accordance with this clause. You agree that You shall be liable to pay any additional fees as determined by Razorpay PA in the event:

You avail certain value-added services available on the dashboard irrespective of whether they have been available free of charge previously.

You avail any new Services not mentioned in these Terms.You agree that Your use of any Service or a value-added service shall be construed as a consent to any additional fees which may be levied by Razorpay on such additional Service or value-added service.

clauses.title: Dynamic currency conversion (DCC)
clauses.verbatim_text: 14.4 An end user may avail dynamic currency conversion ("DCC") services which may be made available by Razorpay PA to an end user, as applicable. The transaction amount payable by the end user towards the purchase of goods or services from a Merchant shall be inclusive of charges for such DCC services availed by the end user. An end user may reach out to dcc_invoice@razorpay.com, by providing the payment ID and end user contact number, to get invoices for DCC transactions.

clauses.title: CNP / recurring / cross-border transactions at Merchant's own risk
clauses.verbatim_text: 14.6 Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies available to Razorpay PA, the Facility Providers, or the Card Networks, You expressly acknowledge and agree that all Transactions processed through the Services, including but not limited to card-not-present transactions (CNP), recurring transactions, and cross-border transactions, shall be undertaken and borne entirely at Your own risk.

clauses.title: Card acceptance prohibitions (min/max, splitting, cash, etc.)
clauses.verbatim_text: 14.7 You undertake and covenant that You shall not (i) impose any minimum or maximum transaction values for any goods or services sold to your customers; (ii) discriminate against the use of cards (or any other payment instrument) as a payment method at Your platform/website/application in any way; (iii) split a single sale into two or more Transactions for the Customer; (iv) accept a Transaction or present transaction data for processing which was not undertaken directly between You and the cardholder/Customer; (v) accept or process transactions in order to give customers cash; (vi) accept transactions relating to goods or services that fall outside the approved description of Your business profile on file with Razorpay PA and the Facility Providers; or (vii) submit any transaction which You know or ought to have known is illegal, fraudulent, or in violation of any Applicable Laws (including PMLA/FEMA); (vii) Discourage or encourage the end Customers from using any payment type or card/card network.

clauses.title: Prohibition on real-money online gaming per 2025 Act
clauses.verbatim_text: 14.10 You expressly covenant and warrant that You shall not use the Services or the Platform for the sale, promotion, facilitation, or acceptance of payments related to any form of real money online gaming or an online money game/online money game service as defined under the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025.

clauses.title: Immediate suspension triggers (breach, unlawful, high-risk, etc.)
clauses.verbatim_text:
16.1 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Razorpay PA shall have the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement of any monies or payments to You, without any liability to You, in the event of the following:

You breach any clause of these Terms.

You facilitate any transaction which is unlawful or in contravention with ‘Prohibited Products and Services' listed below in clause 17 of Part A: General Terms and Conditions.

Razorpay PA receives instructions from Facility Providers or governmental authorities or law enforcement agencies to either suspend the Services, or part thereof, or directs to suspend the Services or part thereof regardless of whether there is pending investigation/enquiry into any alleged illegal/unlawful activities.

You use the Services for any transactions which have a high-risk score as per Razorpay PA's internal fraud assessment tools and other policies.

Razorpay PA is of the opinion that there are suspicious circumstances surrounding Your activities.

Razorpay PA is of the opinion that there are pending, anticipated, or excessive disputes, refunds, or reversals relating to Your use of Services.

Your products/services infringe, or are suspected of infringing, intellectual property rights, copyrightable works, patented inventions, trademarks and trade secrets, or You are suspected of selling counterfeit and/or knock-off goods.

You materially change the type of the products/services provided to end customers and as declared on the onboarding form, without obtaining Razorpay PA's prior written permission to use the Services for the new or changed types of services/products, or it is discovered by Razorpay PA that You provided substantially misleading and/or false information about Your products/services as part of the onboarding activities.

Razorpay PA in its sole discretion determines that Your activities expose Razorpay PA to risks which are unacceptable to Razorpay.

Razorpay PA in its sole discretion is required to do so due to regulatory changes impacting the Services.

clauses.title: Term and termination rights
clauses.verbatim_text: 16.2 These Terms are effective upon the date You first access or use the Platform or Services and continue until such access and use is terminated by You or Razorpay PA. Unless you have a separate offline agreement with Razorpay PA in relation to use the Services, or any part thereof, these Terms will apply. The executed offline agreement shall prevail only for the specific Services and provisions expressly addressed therein. For all other Services, including any Services enabled or used subsequently, these Terms shall apply, until covered under an addendum/amendment executed between the Parties. We may terminate these Terms or close Your account with Razorpay PA at any time for any reason (including, without limitation, for any activity that may create harm or loss to the goodwill of Razorpay PA). Where Applicable Laws require advance notice of termination to be provided, we will, prior to termination, provide You with the required advance notice of termination.

clauses.title: Post-termination obligations; no compensation liability
clauses.verbatim_text: 16.3 Termination does not immediately relieve You of obligations incurred by You under these Terms. Upon termination, You agree to stop using the Services. Your continued or renewed use of the Services after termination serves to renew Your consent to the Terms. In addition, upon termination You understand and agree that we will not be liable to You for compensation, reimbursement, or damages related to Your use of the Services, or any termination or suspension of the Services or deletion of Your information or account data; and You will still liable to us for any fees or fines, or other financial obligation incurred by You or through Your use of the Services prior to termination.

clauses.title: Settlement timelines: Domestic 5 days / International 15 days / PA-CB Outward via Part IB
clauses.verbatim_text:
Subject to Part A: General Terms and Conditions in conjunction with Part I: Specific Terms for Payment Aggregation Services, Razorpay PA shall facilitate collection of online and offline payments for products/services sold by You. You agree that where any settlement amount is less than Rupee 1, Razorpay shall endeavour to, but is not obligated to You, make such settlement.

Subject to Clause 2 and 3 of Part B, Part I of these Terms, Razorpay PA shall settle the Transaction Amount (net of Permissible Deductions) into your account as per timelines specified below in accordance with the Payment Aggregator Guidelines. You acknowledge and agree that the foregoing is subject to credit to / receipt of funds by Razorpay PA in the Escrow Account from our Facility Providers and risk/compliance check of the Transaction by Razorpay and/or Facility Providers.


**Particulars**

**Settlement Timelines**

Domestic

5 (five) days from the date of receipt of Transaction Amount in the Escrow Account

International / PA - CB Inward Transaction

15 (fifteen) days from the date of receipt of Transaction Amount in the Escrow Account

PA-CB Outward Transaction

As specified in Part B, Part IB of these Terms

clauses.title: Recovery of early-settled amounts if not received in Escrow within 3 days
clauses.verbatim_text: c. If Razorpay PA settles the Transaction Amount under these Terms, at an earlier time than agreed above, Razorpay PA shall have an absolute right to recover the Transaction Amount forthwith if the same is not received in the Escrow Account within three (3) Escrow Bank Working Days following the date of the Transaction for any reason whatsoever.

clauses.title: Fee Credits and deduction mechanics
clauses.verbatim_text: e. You may choose to purchase Fee Credits from Razorpay PA in respect of the online payment aggregation Services. It is agreed that in respect of each Transaction, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct an amount equivalent to Razorpay PA fees along with applicable taxes from the Fee Credit. You agree that if sufficient funds are not available in the Fee Credits, then Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct fees along with applicable taxes from the Transaction Amount. “Fee Credits” are the credits using which You may receive the full settlement amount without any fee deduction. For example, if You have a Fee Credit of INR 100 then all the Transactions will be settled in full and the fees for these payments will be deducted from the Fee Credit of INR 100.

clauses.title: Set-off from Transaction Amount and other funds
clauses.verbatim_text: f. You agree that Razorpay PA shall be entitled, at its sole discretion, to recover any amounts from You that are charged to Razorpay PA and/or debited by Facility Providers from accounts maintained by You or any reason attributable to the provision of Services to You by way of deduction from (i) the Transaction Amount to be settled to You and /or (ii) any of Your other funds held by Razorpay PA in the course of providing the Services. In the event such set off or recovery does not fully reimburse Razorpay PA for the liability owed, You shall pay Razorpay PA a sum equal to any shortfall thereof.

clauses.title: Razorpay may withhold settlement and refund to source per regulatory mandate
clauses.verbatim_text: h. Notwithstanding anything set forth in the Terms, You acknowledge, agree and affirm that in the event Razorpay PA in its absolute discretion determines that, for reasons including but not limited to internal decisions or regulatory mandates, it is not feasible or suitable to settle the funds held in the Escrow Account to you, Razorpay PA reserves the right to withhold such settlement and after giving prior notice to you, shall refund the said amount back to the source account from which it was received.

clauses.title: Chargeback liability rests with Merchant; 3-day document submission
clauses.verbatim_text: If a Facility Provider communicates to Razorpay PA the receipt of a Chargeback Request, You will be notified of the Chargeback. You agree that liability for Chargeback, whether domestic or international, under the Terms solely rests with You. You further agree that it is Your sole discretion whether to avail non-3D secure services or not. Subject to availability of funds, Razorpay PA upon receipt of a Chargeback Request shall forthwith deduct Chargeback Amount from the Transaction Amounts, which may be used, based on the decision of the Facility Provider, either to a) process Chargeback in favour of the customer or b) credit to You. For the avoidance of doubt, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct the Chargeback Amount upon receiving a Chargeback claim. You shall be entitled to furnish to Razorpay PA documents and information (“Chargeback Documents”) pertaining to the Transaction associated with the Chargeback Request in order to substantiate (i) the completion of the aforesaid Transaction; and /or; (ii) delivery of goods/services sought by the customer pursuant to the said Transaction. You shall furnish the Chargeback Documents within three (3) calendar days (or such other period specified by the Facility Provider) of receiving notification of the Chargeback Request.

clauses.title: Facility Provider may reverse Chargeback Amount to customer if docs inadequate
clauses.verbatim_text: You agree that (i) if You are unable to furnish Chargeback Documents; and /or; (ii) the Facility Provider is not satisfied with the Chargeback Documents furnished by You, then the Facility Provider shall be entitled to order Razorpay PA to effect a reversal of the debit of the Chargeback Amount associated with the Chargeback such that the said Chargeback Amount is credited to the customer's Payment Instrument.

clauses.title: Recovery of Chargeback Amount via deduction or 7-day debit note
clauses.verbatim_text: Notwithstanding anything in these Terms, if the Facility Providers charge the Chargeback Amount from Razorpay PA then You agree and acknowledge that Razorpay PA is entitled to recover such Chargeback Amount from You by way of deduction from (i) the Transaction Amounts to be settled to You and (ii) any of Your other funds held by Razorpay PA in the course of providing the Services. Provided however, if the available Transaction Amounts or other funds are insufficient for deduction of the Chargeback Amount, then Razorpay PA is entitled to issue a debit note seeking reimbursement of the Chargeback Amount. You shall reimburse the Chargeback Amount within seven (7) days of receipt of the debit note.

clauses.title: 120-day Withholding Term after termination for chargeback coverage
clauses.verbatim_text: On the issuance of notice of termination under the Terms, Razorpay PA reserves the right to withhold from each settlement made during the notice period, a sum computed based on a Stipulated Percentage (defined hereinbelow) for a period of one hundred and twenty (120) days (“Withholding Term”) from the date of termination of these Terms. The sums so withheld shall be utilized towards settlement of Chargebacks. After processing such Chargebacks, Razorpay PA shall transfer the unutilized amounts, if any, to You forthwith upon completion of the Withholding Term. The ‘Stipulated Percentage' is the proportion of the Chargeback Amounts out of the total Transaction Amounts settled during the subsistence of these Terms.

clauses.title: EMI loan cancellation: 7-working-day response window
clauses.verbatim_text: The following applies for Chargebacks associated with EMI products which are supported by Facility Providers. For any loan cancellation requests, You need to respond to Razorpay PA within seven (7) working days with a suitable response. If loan is to be cancelled, then the same needs to be informed to Razorpay PA and if cancellation request is to be declined then You need to provide proof of delivery and justification. For loans which would get cancelled on the basis of Your confirmation, the amount would be recovered from the daily settlement.

clauses.title: Refunds at Merchant's sole discretion, subject to Escrow funds
clauses.verbatim_text: You agree and acknowledge that subject to availability of funds received in the Escrow Account, You are entitled to effect Refunds at Your sole discretion

clauses.title: Refunds processed only on initiation via Platform
clauses.verbatim_text: You further agree and acknowledge that initiation of Refunds is at Your discretion and Razorpay PA shall process a Refund only upon initiation of the same on the Platform.

clauses.title: Refunds routed to original payment method
clauses.verbatim_text: All Refunds initiated by You shall be routed to the same payment method through which the Transaction was processed.

clauses.title: Fees remain payable on Transactions even if refunded
clauses.verbatim_text: You agree that Razorpay PA fees shall always be applicable and payable by You on each Transaction irrespective of whether You have refunded the same to Your customer either through normal channels of refunds or through the instant refund service of Razorpay PA Group Entities (if availed).

clauses.title: Auto-refund within 5 days for late-authorized uncaptured payments
clauses.verbatim_text: You acknowledge and agree that for payments that are late authorized but not captured by You, Razorpay PA may initiate auto-refund to the customer within five (5) days.

clauses.title: Unauthorized debit: suspension of settlements during investigation
clauses.verbatim_text: Subject to clause 2.1 and 2.2 of this Part B, Part I, if Razorpay PA is intimated, by a Facility Provider, that a customer has reported an unauthorised debit of the customer's Payment Instrument (“Fraudulent Transaction”), then in addition to its rights under clause 16 of Part A: General Terms and Conditions, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution thereof by the Facility Providers.

clauses.title: Post-settlement fraud disputes: resolved per RBI circulars
clauses.verbatim_text: If the amount in respect of the Fraudulent Transaction has already been settled to You pursuant to these Terms, any dispute arising in relation to the said Fraudulent Transaction, following settlement, shall be resolved in accordance with the RBI's notification DBR.No.Leg.BC.78/09.07.005/2017-18, dated July 6, 2017 read with RBI's notification DBOD. LEG. BC 86/09.07.007/2001-02 dated April 8, 2002, and other notifications, circulars and guidelines issued by the RBI in this regard from time to time.

clauses.title: Fraudulent Transaction resulting in Chargeback follows chargeback rules
clauses.verbatim_text: Subject to clause 4.2 above, if the Fraudulent Transaction results in a Chargeback, then such Chargeback shall be resolved in accordance with the provisions set out in the Terms.

clauses.title: Merchant liable for UPI fraud threshold breaches per NPCI guideline
clauses.verbatim_text: You shall be liable in the event of breach of the fraud amount thresholds as provided under the NPCI guideline on ‘Fraud liability guidelines on UPI transactions' NPCI/2022- 23/RMD/001. You hereby understand and agree that the decision of the NPCI or the concerned acquiring bank, as the case may be, shall be final and binding.

clauses.title: Survival of clauses 2 and 4 post termination
clauses.verbatim_text: Clauses 2 and 4 (and all sub-clauses thereof) of Part B, Part I shall survive the termination of the Terms.

clauses.title: Compliance with Payment Aggregator Guidelines and data handling
clauses.verbatim_text: You represent and warrant that (i) You shall during the entire term of the usage of the Services, implement, observe and comply with applicable requirements prescribed under Applicable Laws, including but not limited to the provisions of the Payment Aggregator Guidelines. You shall further ensure that Your operations are in compliance with the Payment Aggregator Guidelines and You shall not undertake any action in breach of the same (ii) You shall on Your website/web app/mobile site/mobile app/ other modes of customer interface clearly indicate/display (a) the return and refund policy of Your products/services to Your customers, including the timelines for processing such returns, refunds or cancellations; and (b) the general terms of use and conditions of use by Your customers. You shall ensure that You deliver products and services in accordance with instructions of the customers. (iii) You shall at no time hold, store, copy or keep any customer data relating to a customer's Payment Instrument and shall notify in writing to Razorpay without any delay if You suspect or have become aware of a possible security breach related to any customer data. (iv) You shall not store any data pertaining to the Payment Instrument / customer Payment Instrument credentials or access any database or server containing any data pertaining to Payment Instrument/Payment Instrument credentials. On demand, You shall provide a written confirmation, in a form and manner acceptable to Razorpay PA and Facility Providers, certifying compliance to this aspect.

clauses.title: PCI DSS / PA-DSS compliance and annual reporting
clauses.verbatim_text: You shall comply with or enter into an agreement with a third party service provider of payment processing services for compliance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (“PCI DSS”), as may be amended from time to time, and the Payment Application-Data Security Standard (“PA-DSS”), if applicable. You shall also submit an annual report in writing to Razorpay PA signifying proof of compliance with the above. If You become aware that You will not be or are likely not to be, in compliance with PCI DSS or PA DSS for any reason, You will promptly report in writing to Razorpay such non-compliance or likely non-compliance.

clauses.title: D+4 customer complaint resolution
clauses.verbatim_text: You shall ensure that all its complaints/queries (including with respect to frauds, transactions, Product(s)/Service(s)), are responded to within timelines as may be agreed timelines or as prescribed by RBI/any other regulator/ authority. You shall resolve such matters within D+4 business days (wherein ‘D’ is the date on which the complaint/query is received by You). You shall co-operate with Razorpay PA and/or the Facility Provider(s) to facilitate any investigation/ resolution of Customer queries/ complaints in a reasonable timeframe, including by furnishing transaction details, invoices/ records pertaining to any Customer’s order etc.

clauses.title: Optimizer Services (SaaS routing) - role and fees
clauses.verbatim_text:
*7.1 Optimizer*For the purpose of these services, unless the context otherwise requires:

“Optimizer Services” means a technology solution developed by Razorpay PA which enables You to route Your payments through specific payment gateways based on Your business conditions and preferences. This solution is a software layer on top of Your payment to route every payment request received for Your unique id, based on the rules created by You on Optimizer's merchant facing dashboard.

“Optimizer Transaction” means any transaction routed by You through the Optimizer Services, which involves an order or request placed by the customer with You by paying the Optimizer Transaction Amount to You, while using the services of any payment gateway or payment aggregator.

“Optimizer Transaction Amount” means the amount paid by the customer to You which is then routed by You through the Optimizer Services.

You agree that the Optimizer Services are being provided by Razorpay PA solely as a software as a solution (SaaS) provider. The Optimizer Services are independent and separate from the other Services being provided to You. It is clarified that, for these Optimizer Services, Razorpay PA's role will strictly be that of a SaaS provider and will not be that of a payment service provider.

You hereby consent Razorpay PA to use or process any type of data shared by You for the provision of the Optimizer Services.

Razorpay PA shall have the right to charge additional fees for provision of the Optimizer Services.

You shall for Your omission/commission indemnify and hold Razorpay PA, its directors, managers, officers, employees and agents harmless from and against all losses arising from claims, demands, actions or other proceedings as a result of disputes or claims raised by any payment gateway / payment aggregator in relation to Optimizer Services.

clauses.title: SNRR Account applicability (RBI Master Directions)
clauses.verbatim_text: 8.1 You agree that the following terms shall only apply in case You hold a Special Non-Resident Rupee Account (“SNRR Account”) pursuant to the RBI ‘Master Direction - Deposits and Accounts' dated January 1, 2016, and Circular on ‘Non-resident Rupee Accounts – Review of Policy' dated November 22, 2019, detailed hereinbelow.

clauses.title: SNRR tenure concurrent to contract; max seven years
clauses.verbatim_text: 8.7 The tenure of the SNRR Account shall be concurrent to the tenure of the contract / period of operation / the business of the account holder and in no case shall exceed seven years. Approval of the RBI shall be obtained in cases requiring renewal, provided the restriction of seven years shall not be applicable to SNRR accounts opened for the purposes stated at sub. paragraphs i to v of paragraph 1 of Schedule 4 of Regulation 5(4) of Foreign Exchange Management (Deposit) Regulations, 2016.

clauses.title: Applicability of Part IA to Devices and offline PA
clauses.verbatim_text: 1.1 The terms and conditions set forth in this Part IA apply exclusively to You when You utilize the Devices and/or use offline payment aggregation (PA-P) (defined as facilitating transactions where both the acceptance device and payment instrument are physically present in close proximity while making the transaction). This Part IA shall be read in conjunction with and is supplementary to the Part A (General Terms and Conditions) and Part I (Payment Aggregation Services) of these Terms. All terms and conditions detailed in Part A and Part I remain in full force and effect. In the event of any irreconcilable conflict between the express provisions of this Part IA and those of Part I or Part A, the provisions of this Part IA shall prevail only to the extent necessary to govern the specific requirements of offline payment aggregation. Your access to or use of the Device or offline payment aggregation services confirms Your full, binding acceptance of these additional terms.

clauses.clause_id: Part B Part IA Device Lock-in
clauses.title: 36-month Device lock-in; early termination fee
clauses.verbatim_text: Merchant agrees that each Device ordered by Merchant shall have a minimum period of usage of 36 (thirty-six) months , also referred to as the “Lock-in period” commencing from the date of deployment of such Device at the Merchant location. It is to be clarified that in the event the Merchant deactivates or returns a particular Device or set of Devices, before the expiry of the Lock-in period, Merchant shall make a one-time payment to Razorpay PA of an amount equivalent to the remaining rental or fees (as applicable) for the unexpired duration of the Lock-in Period for such Devices. On termination of Services for whatever reasons, the Merchant shall return all the Devices immediately to Razorpay PA (if taken on rental basis).

clauses.title: 15% late payment interest
clauses.verbatim_text: Charge a late payment interest at the rate of 15% per annum on the due amount

clauses.title: PA-CB Outward settlement timeline: 25 days
clauses.verbatim_text:
1.2 Subject to Clauses 2, 3, 4 and 5 as provided under Part B, Part I of these Terms, Razorpay PA shall settle the Transaction Amount, net of applicable fees, charges, taxes, and other permissible deductions (“Permissible Deductions”), into Your designated account as per timelines specified below in accordance with the Payment Aggregator Guidelines. You acknowledge and agree that the foregoing is subject to credit to / receipt of funds by Razorpay PA in the Escrow Account from our Facility Providers and risk/compliance check of the Transaction by Razorpay and/or Facility Providers

**Particulars**

**Settlement Timelines**

PA-CB Outward Transactions

25 (twenty-five) days from the date of receipt of Transaction Amount in the Escrow Account.

clauses.title: 3-day submission of supporting documents for repatriation
clauses.verbatim_text: 1.3 You shall, within three (3) days from the date of request, provide all necessary transaction-related documents including but not limited to digital invoices, transport documents, and any other information or documentation as may be requested by Razorpay PA at its sole discretion, or as may be required under applicable laws or by Facility Providers (“Supporting Documents”) for every instance of fund repatriation. Delay in submission or discrepancies in the Supporting Documents may result in Razorpay PA rejecting the Transaction and refunding the Transaction Amount to the Customer. You agree that Razorpay PA may rely on the Supporting Documents provided by You without independent verification and may share such documents or related information with Facility Providers, Governmental Authorities, or law enforcement agencies as required under applicable law or upon request.

clauses.title: Withholding taxes and quarterly Tax Withholding Certificates
clauses.verbatim_text: 1.5 Prior to such settlement of funds, Razorpay PA shall, if required, deduct applicable withholding taxes in accordance with prevailing Indian income tax laws. You shall provide such supporting documentation as required under applicable law. Razorpay PA shall issue quarterly Tax Withholding Certificates (i.e. Transaction Based Reports) to enable You to make any tax claims in the relevant overseas jurisdiction.

clauses.title: INR 25,00,000 maximum Transaction value
clauses.verbatim_text: 1.13 The maximum value of a Transaction that can be processed by Razorpay PA is INR 25,00,000/- (Twenty-Five Lakhs).

clauses.title: Liberalised Remittance Scheme additional terms
clauses.verbatim_text:
1.19 If You use the Services for Transactions that fall under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme issued by the Reserve Bank of India (“LRS Guidelines”), the following additional terms apply:

You represent, warrant, and agree that all Transactions under the LRS will fully comply with the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (“FEMA”), the LRS Guidelines, and all applicable RBI regulations, notifications, and circulars, as amended from time to time.

You shall collect from each Customer the LRS declaration and the Tax Collected at Source (“TCS”) declaration in the form and format prescribed or provided by Razorpay, in accordance with Section 206C(1G) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, and other applicable laws. Razorpay may update these forms as laws change, and you agree to implement such changes promptly. Copies of these declarations must be provided to Razorpay.

You must perform appropriate due diligence on your Customers to ensure all Transactions comply with applicable LRS limits and requirements.

You must immediately notify Razorpay in writing if you become aware of any non-compliance, investigation, or regulatory notice relating to Transactions under the LRS.

Razorpay reserves the right to suspend or terminate the processing of any LRS-related Transactions, without liability, if you are suspected or found to be in breach of the LRS Guidelines or any applicable law.

You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Razorpay and/or the AD-1 Bank against any losses, liabilities, damages, costs, expenses (including legal fees), fines, or penalties arising from any misrepresentation, omission, false declaration, or non-compliance by you or your Customers with the LRS Guidelines, FEMA, the Income Tax Act, or any other applicable law or regulation.

clauses.title: Onboarding and Sponsor Bank registration prerequisite for E-Mandate
clauses.verbatim_text:
You shall provide the necessary KYC Documents to Razorpay as specified in this Part II: Specific Terms for E-Mandate Services of Part B (“Mandate Terms”), such that Razorpay can share the KYC Documents (or the information therein) to the Sponsor Bank for the Sponsor Bank's decision on issuing a registration to You to avail of services for e-mandate payments.

You acknowledge that the onboarding and registration process is a prerequisite under the Procedural Guidelines in order for You to avail of Services for e-mandate payments and the customers to be able to start submitting E-Mandate Registration Requests. Razorpay shall not be liable to provide any Services under these Terms until the Sponsor Bank has issued a registration in Your favour.

clauses.clause_id: Part B Part II Withholding Rights
clauses.title: Right to withhold settlements on suspected fraudulent e-mandate transactions
clauses.verbatim_text: In addition to rights under these Terms, if there are reasonable grounds for Razorpay to suspect that a transaction to effect an E-Mandate Payment is done fraudulently or if the Sponsor Bank, NPCI or Destination Bank suspecting so, communicate the same to Razorpay, then Razorpay shall be entitled to withhold settlements to You.

clauses.title: TokenHQ introduction and RBI tokenisation circular compliance
clauses.verbatim_text: You understand and acknowledge that the TokenHQ is an end-to-end solution for You to allow Your customers to continue using the saved cards feature in compliance with RBI's guidelines on tokenisation set out in the Circular CO.DPSS.POLC.No.s-516/02-14-003/2021-22 read with DPSS.CO.PD No.1463/02.14.003/2018-19 dated January 8, 2019 and related guidelines and clarifications issued by the RBI (together “Circular”). For the purposes of these Terms, the Applicable Laws shall be deemed to include the Circular.

clauses.title: Explicit customer consent required for tokenisation (not default-checked)
clauses.verbatim_text:
You acknowledge and agree that in order to allow using the saved card feature, the customer card details must be tokenized. Accordingly, You shall, except in the case of standard checkout:

be solely responsible for obtaining consent of the customer to tokenize (and save) the customer's card. Such consent shall be explicit and not by way of a forced / default / automatic selection of checkbox, radio button, etc.

inform the customers of the purpose of obtaining such consent and that the card will not be tokenized (and saved) if the customer does not provide explicit consent and shall do all things as required to tokenize (and save) the card details pursuant to Applicable Laws.

share such customer consent with Razorpay in order for Razorpay to trigger the additional factor authentication (AFA) with the issuing bank which is required to register the tokenisation request. You acknowledge and agree that if such customer consent is not shared during the payment flow, then Razorpay shall not tokenize (and save) the customer card details.

provide the customer an option to de-register the token and delete the card.

clauses.title: Subscription Services overview and AFA requirement
clauses.verbatim_text:
Subscription services provide You the platform to create and manage subscription plans for Your customers with automated recurring transactions. With this product You can (i) create multiple subscription plans for customers, (ii) automatically charge customers based on a billing cycle that You control, and (iii) get instant alerts on payment activity as well as the status of subscriptions.

You acknowledge and agree that these terms (i) shall apply for debit, credit and prepaid instruments or other methods as notified from time to time (together “Card”) of customers, and (ii) does not apply to once-only / one-time payments.

You acknowledge and agree that:

The customer desirous of opting for e-mandate facility on Card is required to undertake a one-time registration process, with an Additional Factor Authentication (AFA) validation by the issuer bank and that an e-mandate on Card of customers for recurring transactions shall be registered only after successful AFA validation.

Where the first transaction is being performed along with the registration of e- mandate, then AFA validation may be combined. Subsequent recurring transactions shall be performed only for those Card which have been successfully registered and for which the first transaction was successfully authenticated and authorised.

clauses.clause_id: Part B Part VI §2.1 RTO Protection
clauses.title: RTO Protection conditions (Magic Intelligence on, non-prepaid)
clauses.verbatim_text:
2.1 In case You are availing the RTO Protection as a service from Razorpay for a consideration, You shall be entitled to claim reimbursement for, the return shipping fees incurred by You on such orders which have been returned to You (“RTO Protection”) provided that each of the following conditions are met:

You have switched on/accepted the Magic Intelligence (the proprietary technology solution developed by Razorpay which analyses, identifies orders placed by customers that may potentially result into ‘return to origin') feature at all times on Your dashboard.

The return of such orders have been solely due to the Magic Intelligence feature failing to detect/identify such ‘return to origin' order. It is however clarified that any return shipping fees incurred by You on exchange/product return/product refusal after delivery, by customers shall not be covered under RTO Protection.

The orders for which RTO Protection is claimed are not pre-paid orders.
